Feature/User Story: User can submit support ticket with title, description, priority
Acceptance Criteria: Create ticket via POST /tickets; returns 201 with ticket ID; ticket appears in database

Feature/User Story: User can view list of their tickets
Acceptance Criteria: GET /tickets returns tickets filtered by user role; customers see own tickets, reps see assigned tickets
Notes: Role-based filtering critical for data isolation

Feature/User Story: User can view individual ticket details with message thread
Acceptance Criteria: GET /tickets/{id} returns ticket with all messages; messages ordered by timestamp

Feature/User Story: User can add messages to ticket thread
Acceptance Criteria: POST /tickets/{id}/messages adds message; message appears in thread immediately

Feature/User Story: Rep can update ticket status (Open/In Progress/Resolved/Closed)
Acceptance Criteria: PATCH /tickets/{id} updates status; status transitions validated (no skipping states)
Notes: Business logic: cannot reopen closed tickets

Feature/User Story: Rep can assign tickets to other representatives
Acceptance Criteria: POST /tickets/{id}/assign assigns ticket; assigned rep receives notification (future)
Notes: Admin can reassign any ticket; reps can request reassignment

Feature/User Story: User can filter tickets by status, priority, assignment
Acceptance Criteria: Frontend filters by status/priority/assignment; backend query params supported
Notes: Improves rep productivity for large ticket volumes

Feature/User Story: Admin can upload documents (PDF, TXT, MD, DOCX)
Acceptance Criteria: POST /kb/upload accepts files; returns document ID; document metadata stored in app.documents
Notes: File parsing for PDF/DOCX requires libraries (pypdf2, python-docx)

Feature/User Story: System automatically chunks uploaded documents
Acceptance Criteria: Chunking produces 512-1024 token segments with 20% overlap; chunks stored in app.chunks with SHA-256 deduplication
Notes: Configurable chunk size via environment variables

Feature/User Story: System generates embeddings for document chunks
Acceptance Criteria: Google text-embedding-004 generates 768-dim vectors; embeddings stored in FAISS index per organization
Notes: Requires GOOGLE_API_KEY; async processing for large documents

Feature/User Story: System stores embeddings in FAISS vector index
Acceptance Criteria: FAISS IndexFlatIP (cosine similarity) per organization; indices saved to /data/faiss/{org_id}/kb.index

Feature/User Story: Admin can view knowledge base statistics (documents, chunks, citations)
Acceptance Criteria: GET /kb/stats returns document count, total chunks, average chunks per document

Feature/User Story: Admin can delete documents from knowledge base
Acceptance Criteria: DELETE /kb/documents/{id} removes document, chunks, and embeddings; FAISS index reindexed

Feature/User Story: Rep can open AI assistant modal from ticket interface
Acceptance Criteria: AI modal overlays ticket page; no page navigation required
Notes: UX: modal positioned prominently; keyboard shortcut (Ctrl+K)

Feature/User Story: Rep can ask AI assistant questions in natural language
Acceptance Criteria: POST /rep/chat accepts query; returns AI response with confidence score and citations in <5s
Notes: Backend orchestrates: embedding → FAISS search → Gemini generation

Feature/User Story: System generates 7-factor confidence score for AI responses
Acceptance Criteria: Confidence score (0-100%) includes: retrieval quality (30%), citation coverage (20%), semantic coherence (20%), completeness (10%), density (10%), diversity (10%), variance penalty

Feature/User Story: System displays confidence score with visual indicators (green/yellow/red)
Acceptance Criteria: Confidence >80%: green; 60-80%: yellow; <60%: red; warning message for red scores

Feature/User Story: System provides automatic escalation recommendation when confidence <55%
Acceptance Criteria: 6-signal escalation logic: low confidence, insufficient context, high uncertainty, long conversations, complex queries, critical phrases

Feature/User Story: AI responses include mandatory citations to source documents
Acceptance Criteria: Prompt engineering enforces [Source: Document X] format; citations hyperlinked to KB documents
Notes: Citation parser extracts document IDs from response text

Feature/User Story: Rep can copy AI response to clipboard for pasting into ticket
Acceptance Criteria: One-click copy button; clipboard API integration; success toast notification
Notes: UX: Copy button prominent near AI response

Feature/User Story: Rep can provide feedback (thumbs up/down) on AI responses
Acceptance Criteria: POST /feedback submits rating; feedback stored in app.ai_feedback with ticket context
Notes: Enables continuous improvement and confidence validation

Feature/User Story: Admin can view organization-wide ticket volume metrics
Acceptance Criteria: Dashboard displays: total tickets, tickets by status, tickets by priority, trend charts

Feature/User Story: Admin can view representative performance metrics
Acceptance Criteria: Dashboard shows: tickets handled per rep, avg resolution time, AI usage per rep

Feature/User Story: Admin can view RAG usage analytics (confidence distributions, low-confidence queries)
Acceptance Criteria: Dashboard displays: confidence score distribution, list of queries with confidence <60%, document citation frequency
Notes: Identifies knowledge gaps for documentation improvement

Feature/User Story: Admin can export analytics reports (CSV)
Acceptance Criteria: Export button generates CSV with selected date range and metrics

Feature/User Story: User can create new organization
Acceptance Criteria: POST /organizations creates org; user automatically assigned as owner role
Notes: First-time users auto-provisioned with default organization

Feature/User Story: User can switch between organizations
Acceptance Criteria: OrganizationSelector dropdown in frontend; X-Organization-ID header sent with all API requests
Notes: Frontend context management via React Context API

Feature/User Story: User can invite members to organization
Acceptance Criteria: POST /organizations/{org_id}/members sends invite (future: email); invited user gains access after accepting

Feature/User Story: System enforces data isolation via PostgreSQL RLS
Acceptance Criteria: All queries filtered by organization_id; 20+ RLS policies across 12 tables; zero cross-tenant data leakage
Notes: Critical security requirement; validated via penetration testing

Feature/User Story: System maintains separate FAISS indices per organization
Acceptance Criteria: FAISS indices stored in /data/faiss/{org_id}/kb.index; lazy loading on first query

Feature/User Story: System validates JWT tokens on all protected endpoints
Acceptance Criteria: JWT verification with Supabase public key; issuer and expiry checks; invalid tokens return 401

Feature/User Story: System enforces rate limiting (10-200 req/min by endpoint)
Acceptance Criteria: SlowAPI integration: auth (10/min), AI (10/min), create (20/min), general (100/min), read (200/min); 429 status on exceeded limits
Notes: IP-based rate limiting; future: user-based limits

Feature/User Story: System applies security headers to all responses
Acceptance Criteria: SecurityHeadersMiddleware adds: CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy
Notes: Protects against XSS, clickjacking, MIME sniffing

Feature/User Story: System validates X-Organization-ID header on all org-scoped endpoints
Acceptance Criteria: OrganizationContextMiddleware extracts and validates org_id; verifies user membership; rejects unauthorized access

Feature/User Story: Automated tests run on every pull request
Acceptance Criteria: GitHub Actions workflow: ci-development.yml; frontend and backend tests run in parallel; 100% pass rate required for merge
Notes: 11-step quality pipeline: TypeScript, ESLint, Prettier, pytest, etc.

Feature/User Story: Code quality checks (linting, formatting) enforced
Acceptance Criteria: Frontend: ESLint, Prettier; Backend: Black, isort; failures block PR merge

Feature/User Story: Security vulnerability scanning on dependencies
Acceptance Criteria: npm audit (frontend), Snyk (frontend), Bandit (backend), Safety (backend); critical vulnerabilities block deployment

Feature/User Story: Automated deployment to staging and production
Acceptance Criteria: deploy-staging.yml triggers on develop branch push; deploy-production.yml triggers on main branch tag; Vercel (frontend) + Railway (backend)
Notes: Environment-specific secrets managed via GitHub Secrets

Feature/User Story: API response times <200ms for CRUD operations
Acceptance Criteria: Ticket creation, status updates, message posting complete in <200ms; database queries optimized with indexes

Feature/User Story: RAG query latency <5s end-to-end
Acceptance Criteria: Embedding generation + FAISS search + Gemini generation completes in <5s; timeout at 30s

Feature/User Story: Database query latency <100ms for complex analytics
Acceptance Criteria: Admin analytics queries (ticket volume, rep performance) complete in <100ms; uses materialized views or query optimization

Feature/User Story: System supports 100+ concurrent organizations without degradation
Acceptance Criteria: Load testing with 100 orgs; no >20% latency increase; horizontal scaling via backend replicas
